properties.name: 1976 Tangshan Earthquake
properties.description: Devastating earthquake that struck the industrial city of Tangshan in the early morning, destroying most of the city and causing one of the deadliest earthquakes in modern history.

properties.name: 1976 Guatemala Earthquake
properties.description: Major strike-slip earthquake along the Motagua Fault that devastated Guatemala City and surrounding areas, leaving over a million homeless.

properties.name: 1978 Tabas Earthquake
properties.description: Powerful earthquake that destroyed the city of Tabas in eastern Iran, killing most of its population and flattening nearly every structure.

properties.name: 1980 El Asnam Earthquake
properties.description: Destructive earthquake in northern Algeria that destroyed much of the city of El Asnam, triggering landslides and significant ground deformation.

properties.name: 1980 Irpinia Earthquake
properties.description: Strong earthquake in southern Italy that caused widespread destruction across the Campania and Basilicata regions, exposing construction deficiencies.

properties.name: 1985 Mexico City Earthquake
properties.description: Massive subduction earthquake off the coast of Michoacán that caused catastrophic damage in Mexico City due to soft lake-bed soils amplifying seismic waves.

properties.name: 1986 San Salvador Earthquake
properties.description: Shallow earthquake directly beneath San Salvador that caused disproportionate damage and casualties relative to its moderate magnitude.

properties.name: 1988 Spitak Earthquake
properties.description: Devastating earthquake in northern Armenia that destroyed the city of Spitak and severely damaged Leninakan, prompting an international relief effort during the Soviet era.

properties.name: 1989 Loma Prieta Earthquake
properties.description: Major earthquake that struck during the World Series, collapsing a section of the Bay Bridge and the Cypress Street Viaduct in Oakland.

properties.name: 1990 Manjil-Rudbar Earthquake
properties.description: Destructive earthquake in northern Iran that obliterated the cities of Manjil and Rudbar, causing massive landslides in the Alborz mountain region.

properties.name: 1990 Luzon Earthquake
properties.description: Powerful earthquake on the Philippine Fault that caused extensive damage in Baguio City and Cabanatuan, triggering massive landslides.

properties.name: 1992 Flores Earthquake
properties.description: Major earthquake and subsequent tsunami that struck Flores Island, devastating coastal communities and causing massive destruction on Babi Island.

properties.name: 1994 Northridge Earthquake
properties.description: Blind thrust earthquake that struck the San Fernando Valley, causing widespread structural damage and becoming one of the costliest natural disasters in US history.

properties.name: 1995 Great Hanshin (Kobe) Earthquake
properties.description: Devastating shallow earthquake that struck Kobe, causing catastrophic damage to infrastructure and triggering fires across the densely populated port city.

properties.name: 1997 Qayen Earthquake
properties.description: Strong earthquake in eastern Iran that destroyed numerous villages and the town of Birjand, occurring in a sparsely populated but vulnerable region.

properties.name: 1998 Afghanistan Earthquake
properties.description: Series of strong earthquakes in remote northeastern Afghanistan that devastated villages in Takhar Province, with rescue efforts hampered by the ongoing civil war.

properties.name: 1999 Izmit Earthquake
properties.description: Catastrophic earthquake along the North Anatolian Fault that devastated the industrial heartland of Turkey, causing fires at the Tüpraş oil refinery.

properties.name: 1999 Chi-Chi Earthquake
properties.description: Major thrust earthquake in central Taiwan that ruptured along the Chelungpu fault, causing massive ground displacement and widespread building collapses.

properties.name: 1999 Düzce Earthquake
properties.description: Strong earthquake on the North Anatolian Fault occurring three months after the Izmit earthquake, further devastating northwestern Turkey.

properties.name: 2001 Gujarat Earthquake
properties.description: Massive intraplate earthquake on India's Republic Day that devastated the city of Bhuj and surrounding villages in the Kutch district of Gujarat.

properties.name: 2001 El Salvador Earthquake
properties.description: Major earthquake off the coast of El Salvador that triggered a massive landslide in Santa Tecla, burying hundreds of homes.

properties.name: 2003 Bam Earthquake
properties.description: Devastating earthquake that destroyed the ancient city of Bam, including its historic 2,000-year-old Arg-e Bam citadel, killing a quarter of the population.

properties.name: 2004 Sumatra-Andaman Earthquake
properties.description: Megathrust earthquake that triggered a catastrophic Indian Ocean tsunami devastating coastlines across 14 countries, one of the deadliest natural disasters in recorded history.

properties.name: 2005 Kashmir Earthquake
properties.description: Powerful earthquake in the disputed Kashmir region that devastated Muzaffarabad and Balakot, collapsing thousands of buildings and triggering massive landslides.

properties.name: 2005 Nias-Simeulue Earthquake
properties.description: Massive aftershock of the 2004 event off the west coast of Sumatra, causing further destruction on Nias and Simeulue islands.

properties.name: 2006 Yogyakarta Earthquake
properties.description: Shallow earthquake near Yogyakarta that destroyed thousands of homes and historic temples in one of Java's most densely populated regions.

properties.name: 2006 Kuril Islands Earthquake
properties.description: Major subduction earthquake in the Kuril Islands chain that generated a minor tsunami, but caused no casualties due to the remote and uninhabited location.

properties.name: 2007 Peru Earthquake
properties.description: Major earthquake near the coast of central Peru that devastated the city of Pisco, collapsing a cathedral during evening Mass.

properties.name: 2007 Sumatra Earthquakes
properties.description: Pair of massive earthquakes off the coast of southern Sumatra that triggered tsunami warnings across the Indian Ocean but caused limited casualties.

properties.name: 2008 Sichuan Earthquake
properties.description: Catastrophic earthquake in Sichuan Province that caused massive destruction across a wide area, with controversial collapses of school buildings sparking public outcry.

properties.name: 2009 L'Aquila Earthquake
properties.description: Destructive earthquake that struck the medieval city of L'Aquila, causing widespread collapse of historic stone buildings and leading to a controversial trial of seismologists.

properties.name: 2009 Sumatra Earthquake
properties.description: Strong earthquake off the coast of Padang that caused severe damage in the city and triggered landslides that buried villages in the surrounding highlands.

properties.name: 2010 Haiti Earthquake
properties.description: Catastrophic shallow earthquake that struck near the capital Port-au-Prince, causing unprecedented destruction in a country with extremely vulnerable infrastructure.

properties.name: 2010 Chile Earthquake
properties.description: Massive megathrust earthquake off central Chile that generated a destructive tsunami, shifted the city of Concepción 3 meters to the west.

properties.name: 2010 Yushu Earthquake
properties.description: Strong earthquake on the Tibetan Plateau that devastated the remote town of Jiegu, collapsing most structures built of traditional materials.

properties.name: 2010 Canterbury (Darfield) Earthquake
properties.description: Powerful earthquake west of Christchurch on a previously unknown fault that caused significant property damage but remarkably no direct fatalities.

properties.name: 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.description: Most powerful earthquake ever recorded in Japan, triggering a massive tsunami that devastated coastal cities and caused the Fukushima nuclear disaster.

properties.name: 2011 Christchurch Earthquake
properties.description: Devastating shallow aftershock of the 2010 Canterbury earthquake that struck directly under Christchurch during lunchtime, destroying the city's central business district.

properties.name: 2011 Van Earthquake
properties.description: Strong earthquake in eastern Turkey that destroyed thousands of buildings in Van and the town of Erciş, followed by a deadly magnitude 5.6 aftershock.

properties.name: 2012 Indian Ocean Earthquake
properties.region: Off coast of Sumatra, Indian Ocean
properties.description: Largest recorded strike-slip earthquake in history that occurred off the coast of Sumatra, triggering brief tsunami warnings but causing minimal damage.

properties.name: 2013 Balochistan Earthquake
properties.description: Major earthquake in remote Balochistan that created a new island off the coast of Gwadar through a mud volcano eruption and destroyed thousands of homes.

properties.name: 2013 Bohol Earthquake
properties.description: Strong earthquake in the Central Visayas that damaged numerous historic churches including the Basilica Minore del Santo Niño and displaced hundreds of thousands.

properties.name: 2014 Ludian Earthquake
properties.description: Shallow earthquake in mountainous Yunnan Province that triggered landslides and destroyed the town of Longtoushan, trapping thousands under rubble.

properties.name: 2014 Iquique Earthquake
properties.description: Major subduction earthquake off northern Chile in the seismic gap zone, generating a small tsunami but causing remarkably few casualties due to strong building codes.

properties.name: 2015 Nepal Earthquake
properties.description: Devastating earthquake that struck central Nepal, destroying historic monuments in Kathmandu's Durbar Square and triggering a deadly avalanche on Mount Everest.

properties.name: 2015 Illapel Earthquake
properties.description: Powerful subduction earthquake off central Chile that triggered a tsunami with waves up to 4.7 meters, prompting coastal evacuations.

properties.name: 2016 Ecuador Earthquake
properties.description: Powerful earthquake that struck the northwestern coast of Ecuador, devastating the towns of Pedernales and Muisne and causing severe damage in Portoviejo.

properties.name: 2016 Kumamoto Earthquakes
properties.description: Sequence of powerful earthquakes that severely damaged Kumamoto Castle and surrounding areas, with the main shock preceded by a strong foreshock two days earlier.

properties.name: 2016 Central Italy Earthquake
properties.description: Destructive earthquake that struck central Italy, nearly destroying the historic town of Amatrice and several surrounding villages in the Apennine mountains.

properties.name: 2017 Chiapas Earthquake
properties.description: Most powerful earthquake in Mexico in a century, striking off the coast of Chiapas and devastating the town of Juchitán de Zaragoza in Oaxaca.

properties.name: 2017 Puebla-Mexico City Earthquake
properties.description: Devastating earthquake on the anniversary of the 1985 disaster that collapsed buildings across Mexico City and Puebla, including a school where 19 children died.

properties.name: 2017 Iran-Iraq Border Earthquake
properties.description: Powerful earthquake along the Iran-Iraq border in the Zagros Mountains that caused widespread destruction in Kermanshah Province and was felt across the Middle East.

properties.name: 2018 Lombok Earthquake
properties.description: Series of destructive earthquakes on Lombok island that caused massive damage in northern Lombok and triggered landslides on Mount Rinjani.

properties.name: 2018 Sulawesi Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.description: Devastating earthquake followed by an unexpected tsunami and soil liquefaction in Palu, causing entire neighborhoods to be swept away by flowing ground.

properties.name: 2018 Anchorage Earthquake
properties.description: Strong intraslab earthquake that caused significant infrastructure damage including road collapses in Anchorage, but no fatalities due to robust building codes.

properties.name: 2019 Ridgecrest Earthquake
properties.description: Largest earthquake in Southern California in 20 years that created a visible surface rupture in the Mojave Desert and caused damage in the town of Ridgecrest.

properties.name: 2019 Albania Earthquake
properties.description: Strongest earthquake to hit Albania in decades, causing building collapses in Durrës and Thumanë and the deadliest earthquake in the country since 1979.

properties.name: 2020 Aegean Sea Earthquake
properties.description: Strong earthquake in the Aegean Sea that caused building collapses in Izmir and a small tsunami that flooded the Greek island of Samos.

properties.name: 2021 Haiti Earthquake
properties.description: Major earthquake in southern Haiti that devastated the city of Les Cayes and surrounding areas, with rescue efforts hampered by Tropical Storm Grace.

properties.name: 2021 Assam Earthquake
properties.description: Strong earthquake in northeastern India that caused cracks in buildings and triggered landslides, the largest in the region since 1950.

properties.name: 2022 Afghanistan Earthquake
properties.description: Deadly shallow earthquake in southeastern Afghanistan that destroyed villages in remote mountainous terrain under Taliban governance, complicating international aid delivery.

properties.name: 2023 Turkey-Syria Earthquake
properties.description: Catastrophic earthquake doublet that struck southeastern Turkey and northern Syria, causing unprecedented destruction across 11 provinces and becoming Turkey's deadliest modern disaster.

properties.name: 2023 Morocco Earthquake
properties.description: Strongest earthquake in Morocco in over a century that devastated villages in the High Atlas Mountains and damaged historic structures in Marrakech.

properties.name: 2023 Herat Earthquakes
properties.description: Sequence of four strong earthquakes in Herat Province over two weeks that flattened entire villages, killing thousands in one of the most earthquake-prone regions.

properties.name: 2024 Noto Peninsula Earthquake
properties.description: Powerful earthquake on New Year's Day that devastated the Noto Peninsula, triggering a tsunami, fires in Wajima, and widespread building collapses.

properties.name: 1976 Moro Gulf Earthquake
properties.description: Massive earthquake and tsunami that struck the Moro Gulf near Mindanao, devastating coastal communities along the Cotabato coast.

properties.name: 1977 Vrancea Earthquake
properties.description: Deep-focus earthquake in the Vrancea seismic zone that caused catastrophic damage in Bucharest, collapsing numerous apartment buildings.

properties.name: 1983 Coalinga Earthquake
properties.description: Blind thrust earthquake that severely damaged the downtown area of Coalinga in the San Joaquin Valley, destroying many unreinforced masonry buildings.

properties.name: 1985 Valparaíso Earthquake
properties.description: Major subduction earthquake that struck central Chile causing widespread damage in Valparaíso and Santiago, and generating a tsunami along the coast.

properties.name: 1999 Armenia (Colombia) Earthquake
properties.description: Shallow earthquake in the Colombian Coffee Axis that devastated the city of Armenia and surrounding municipalities in Quindío Department.

properties.name: 2002 Hindu Kush Earthquake
properties.description: Deep earthquake in the Hindu Kush region that caused destruction across northern Afghanistan, adding to devastation during the post-Taliban transition period.

properties.name: 1993 Latur Earthquake
properties.description: Devastating intraplate earthquake in the stable continental region of peninsular India that destroyed the town of Killari, surprising seismologists.

properties.name: 2015 Hindu Kush Earthquake
properties.description: Deep earthquake in the Hindu Kush mountains felt across South Asia and the Middle East, causing casualties in Afghanistan, Pakistan, and India.

properties.name: 2016 Kaikōura Earthquake
properties.description: Complex earthquake that ruptured at least 12 faults simultaneously, isolating the town of Kaikōura by destroying road and rail links.

properties.name: 2024 Hualien Earthquake
properties.description: Strongest earthquake in Taiwan in 25 years that caused building collapses and landslides in Hualien, tilting the Uranus Building dramatically.

properties.name: 1987 Ecuador Earthquakes
properties.description: Two powerful earthquakes in the Amazon region that triggered massive landslides destroying a section of the Trans-Ecuadorian oil pipeline.

properties.name: 1991 Limón Earthquake
properties.description: Major shallow earthquake on the Caribbean coast of Costa Rica that uplifted coral reefs, destroyed bridges, and caused significant coastal changes.

properties.name: 2003 Boumerdès Earthquake
properties.description: Destructive earthquake east of Algiers that caused widespread building collapses and a tsunami in the western Mediterranean Sea.

properties.name: 2025 Myanmar Earthquake
properties.description: Powerful earthquake in central Myanmar that caused massive destruction in Mandalay and surrounding areas, collapsing thousands of buildings.

properties.name: 1979 Tumaco Earthquake
properties.description: Major subduction earthquake and tsunami on the Pacific coast of Colombia that devastated Tumaco and surrounding coastal communities.

properties.name: Hurricane Helene
properties.description: Helene made landfall in Florida's Big Bend region before tracking inland and causing catastrophic flooding across the southern Appalachians, devastating western North Carolina.

properties.name: Hurricane Milton
properties.description: Milton rapidly intensified in the Gulf of Mexico to Category 5 strength before making landfall near Siesta Key, Florida as a Category 3, causing significant storm surge and tornado outbreaks.

properties.name: Typhoon Yagi
properties.description: Yagi was the strongest typhoon to hit Vietnam in 30 years, causing massive flooding and landslides across northern Vietnam, Laos, and Myanmar.

properties.name: Cyclone Freddy
properties.description: Freddy became the longest-lasting tropical cyclone on record at 36 days, crossing the Indian Ocean twice and making multiple landfalls in southeastern Africa.

properties.name: Hurricane Otis
properties.description: Otis underwent unprecedented rapid intensification from a tropical storm to Category 5 in just 12 hours before making a direct hit on Acapulco, causing catastrophic damage.

properties.name: Hurricane Ian
properties.description: Ian made landfall near Cayo Costa, Florida as a powerful Category 4 hurricane, causing catastrophic storm surge in Fort Myers and widespread devastation across central Florida.

properties.name: Typhoon Noru
properties.description: Noru rapidly intensified before striking Luzon in the Philippines, then re-emerged into the South China Sea and made a second landfall in central Vietnam.

properties.name: Hurricane Ida
properties.description: Ida made landfall in Louisiana on the 16th anniversary of Hurricane Katrina, devastating the region before its remnants caused historic flooding in the northeastern United States.

properties.name: Cyclone Amphan
properties.description: Amphan was the strongest cyclone to form in the Bay of Bengal since 1999, causing massive destruction in West Bengal and Bangladesh with record storm surge.

properties.name: Hurricane Eta
properties.description: Eta devastated Central America with extreme rainfall and catastrophic flooding, particularly in Honduras and Guatemala, before later impacting Cuba and Florida.

properties.name: Hurricane Iota
properties.description: Iota struck nearly the same area of Nicaragua as Hurricane Eta just two weeks prior, becoming the strongest hurricane of the 2020 season and the latest Category 5 on record in the Atlantic.

properties.name: Hurricane Dorian
properties.description: Dorian devastated the Abaco Islands and Grand Bahama, stalling for over 24 hours and producing catastrophic storm surge. It was the strongest hurricane on record to strike the Bahamas.

properties.name: Cyclone Idai
properties.description: Idai was one of the deadliest cyclones in the Southern Hemisphere, causing catastrophic flooding in Mozambique, Zimbabwe, and Malawi. Beira, Mozambique was nearly destroyed.

properties.name: Typhoon Mangkhut
properties.description: Mangkhut was the strongest typhoon of 2018, striking northern Luzon before barreling into Hong Kong and southern China, causing widespread landslides and flooding.

properties.name: Hurricane Michael
properties.description: Michael made landfall near Mexico Beach, Florida as a Category 5 hurricane, the first to strike the US at that intensity since Andrew in 1992. It caused catastrophic damage to the Florida Panhandle.

properties.name: Hurricane Florence
properties.description: Florence stalled near the Carolina coast, dumping record-breaking rainfall that caused catastrophic inland flooding across North and South Carolina.

properties.name: Hurricane Harvey
properties.description: Harvey stalled over southeast Texas for days, producing unprecedented rainfall totals exceeding 1.5 meters and catastrophic flooding in the Houston metropolitan area.

properties.name: Hurricane Irma
properties.description: Irma maintained Category 5 intensity for 60 consecutive hours, the longest on record in the Atlantic. It devastated Barbuda, St. Martin, and the Virgin Islands before striking Florida.

properties.name: Hurricane Maria
properties.description: Maria devastated Dominica and Puerto Rico, causing the worst natural disaster on record for both islands. Puerto Rico's power grid was completely destroyed, leaving the island without electricity for months.

properties.name: Hurricane Matthew
properties.description: Matthew was a catastrophic Category 5 hurricane that devastated southwestern Haiti, killing over 500 people, before tracking along the US East Coast causing significant flooding.

properties.name: Cyclone Pam
properties.description: Pam was one of the most intense tropical cyclones in South Pacific history, causing widespread devastation across Vanuatu, destroying 90% of structures on some islands.

properties.name: Hurricane Patricia
properties.description: Patricia became the strongest hurricane ever recorded in the Western Hemisphere with sustained winds of 345 km/h, making landfall in a sparsely populated area of Jalisco, Mexico.

properties.name: Typhoon Haiyan
properties.description: Haiyan (local name Yolanda) was one of the strongest tropical cyclones ever recorded, devastating the Visayas region with catastrophic storm surge that obliterated Tacloban City.

properties.name: Hurricane Sandy
properties.region: US East Coast / New Jersey / New York
properties.description: Sandy merged with an extratropical system to become a superstorm with an enormous wind field, causing devastating storm surge flooding in New York City and the New Jersey coast.

properties.name: Cyclone Yasi
properties.description: Yasi was the most powerful cyclone to hit Queensland since 1918, making landfall near Mission Beach with destructive winds and storm surge, though mass evacuations kept casualties low.

properties.name: Cyclone Nargis
properties.description: Nargis was the deadliest natural disaster in Myanmar's history, causing catastrophic storm surge flooding in the densely populated Irrawaddy Delta region.

properties.name: Hurricane Katrina
properties.description: Katrina caused catastrophic levee failures in New Orleans and total destruction along the Mississippi coast, becoming the costliest natural disaster in US history at the time.

properties.name: Hurricane Rita
properties.description: Rita became the fourth-most intense Atlantic hurricane ever recorded before making landfall near the Texas-Louisiana border, causing significant storm surge and refinery damage.

properties.name: Hurricane Wilma
properties.description: Wilma was the most intense Atlantic hurricane on record with a pressure of 882 hPa, devastating Cancún before crossing Florida.

properties.name: Hurricane Ivan
properties.description: Ivan was a long-lived and powerful Cape Verde hurricane that devastated Grenada before making landfall in Alabama. Its remnants later looped back into the Gulf of Mexico.

properties.name: Hurricane Jeanne
properties.description: Jeanne caused catastrophic flooding in Haiti, particularly around Gonaïves where mudslides killed thousands, before later striking Florida as a Category 3 hurricane.

properties.name: Hurricane Mitch
properties.description: Mitch was the deadliest Atlantic hurricane since 1780, stalling over Central America and dumping catastrophic rainfall that caused massive floods and mudslides in Honduras and Nicaragua.

properties.name: Hurricane Andrew
properties.description: Andrew was a compact but extremely powerful hurricane that devastated Homestead, Florida, leaving 250,000 people homeless and becoming the costliest US natural disaster at the time.

properties.name: Bangladesh Cyclone (02B)
properties.description: The 1991 Bangladesh cyclone produced a 6-meter storm surge that inundated the low-lying coastal areas of Chittagong, killing over 138,000 people in one of history's deadliest tropical cyclones.

properties.name: Hurricane Hugo
properties.description: Hugo devastated the US Virgin Islands and struck South Carolina as a Category 4, causing massive destruction in Charleston and becoming the costliest US hurricane at the time.

properties.name: Hurricane Gilbert
properties.description: Gilbert was the most intense Atlantic hurricane ever recorded at the time with a pressure of 888 hPa, devastating Jamaica and the Yucatan Peninsula.

properties.name: Hurricane Allen
properties.description: Allen reached Category 5 intensity three separate times and held record maximum sustained winds of 305 km/h for an Atlantic hurricane until surpassed decades later.

properties.name: Hurricane David
properties.description: David was a devastating Category 5 hurricane that struck Dominica and the Dominican Republic before tracking along the US East Coast, causing over 2,000 fatalities.

properties.name: Typhoon Olga
properties.description: Typhoon Olga struck the Philippines in 1976, causing significant flooding and destruction across Luzon.

properties.name: Hurricane Tomas
properties.description: Tomas caused deadly flooding and mudslides in earthquake-ravaged Haiti and damaged Saint Lucia and Saint Vincent.

properties.name: Hurricane Odile
properties.description: Odile was the strongest hurricane to make landfall on the Baja California Peninsula in recorded history, devastating Cabo San Lucas.

properties.name: Odisha Super Cyclone
properties.description: The 1999 Odisha cyclone was the strongest to strike India in the 20th century, with a massive storm surge devastating coastal Odisha and killing nearly 10,000 people.

properties.name: Cyclone Sidr
properties.description: Sidr made landfall in Bangladesh with devastating storm surge, destroying large areas of the Sundarbans mangrove forest and causing thousands of deaths along the coast.

properties.name: Typhoon Morakot
properties.description: Morakot caused the worst flooding in Taiwan in 50 years, burying the village of Xiaolin in a massive mudslide and killing over 600 people.

properties.name: Hurricane Gloria
properties.description: Gloria was the first significant hurricane to strike the northeastern US since 1960, making landfall on Long Island and causing widespread damage across New England.

properties.name: Hurricane Isabel
properties.description: Isabel was the most significant hurricane to affect the Mid-Atlantic region in decades, causing record storm surge in the Chesapeake Bay and severe flooding in Virginia and North Carolina.

properties.name: Cyclone Winston
properties.description: Winston was the strongest cyclone on record in the Southern Hemisphere, making a direct hit on Fiji with devastating winds and storm surge.

properties.name: Typhoon Goni (Rolly)
properties.description: Goni was the strongest landfalling tropical cyclone on record globally, striking the Bicol region of the Philippines with catastrophic 315 km/h winds.

properties.name: Hurricane Alicia
properties.description: Alicia made landfall near Galveston as a Category 3 hurricane, causing significant damage in Houston, including shattering glass facades in the downtown skyline.

properties.name: Hurricane Anita
properties.description: Anita was the first Category 5 hurricane in the Atlantic basin since Camille in 1969, making landfall in a sparsely populated area of Tamaulipas, Mexico.

properties.name: Tropical Storm Alfred
properties.description: Alfred brought record rainfall and severe flooding to Far North Queensland in early 2025, disrupting communities across the Cairns region.

properties.name: Palisades Fire
properties.description: Devastating wildfire that swept through Pacific Palisades and surrounding neighborhoods in January 2025, destroying thousands of structures in one of Los Angeles' most destructive fire events.

properties.name: Eaton Fire
properties.description: Destructive wildfire that burned through Altadena and the foothills above Pasadena in January 2025, destroying over 7,000 structures.

properties.name: Jasper Wildfire
properties.description: Massive wildfire that swept into the town of Jasper in July 2024, destroying roughly a third of the town's structures within Jasper National Park.

properties.name: Park Fire
properties.description: California's fourth-largest wildfire in state history, burning across Butte and Tehama counties from July to August 2024.

properties.name: Maui Wildfires (Lahaina Fire)
properties.description: Deadliest US wildfire in over a century. Wind-driven fire devastated the historic town of Lahaina on August 8, 2023, destroying nearly 2,200 structures.

properties.name: 2023 Canadian Wildfire Season
properties.region: Multiple provinces (Quebec, BC, Alberta, NWT, Ontario)
properties.description: Record-breaking Canadian wildfire season that burned over 45 million acres, more than double any previously recorded season. Smoke blanketed much of North America.

properties.name: Central Chile Wildfires
properties.description: Devastating wildfires in south-central Chile during the 2023 summer, killing at least 26 people and destroying thousands of homes.

properties.name: Hermits Peak / Calf Canyon Fire
properties.description: Largest wildfire in New Mexico history, caused by a US Forest Service prescribed burn that escaped control. Burned from April to August 2022.

properties.name: Dixie Fire
properties.description: Largest single (non-complex) wildfire in California history. Burned for over three months and destroyed the town of Greenville.

properties.name: 2021 Turkey Wildfires
properties.region: Antalya and Muğla provinces, Mediterranean coast
properties.description: Turkey's worst wildfire season on record. Over 200 fires broke out across southern Turkey in July–August 2021 during an extreme heatwave.

properties.name: 2020 US West Coast Fire Siege
properties.description: Record-breaking wildfire season across the US West Coast. Simultaneous large fires in California, Oregon, and Washington burned millions of acres and created hazardous smoke conditions.

properties.name: Pantanal Fires
properties.region: Pantanal wetlands, Mato Grosso do Sul
properties.description: Record fires burned roughly 30% of the world's largest tropical wetland, the Pantanal, devastating wildlife habitat.

properties.name: Black Summer Bushfires
properties.region: New South Wales, Victoria, South Australia, Queensland
properties.description: Catastrophic bushfire season from September 2019 to March 2020 across southeastern Australia. Killed an estimated 3 billion animals and destroyed over 3,000 homes.

properties.name: 2019 Amazon Rainforest Fires
properties.description: Surge of fires in the Amazon in August 2019, driven by deforestation and drought. Over 80,000 fires detected by satellite, drawing global attention.

properties.name: 2019 Siberian Wildfires
properties.description: Massive wildfires across Siberia's boreal forest in summer 2019, releasing large amounts of CO₂ and blanketing cities in smoke.

properties.name: Camp Fire
properties.description: Deadliest and most destructive wildfire in California history. Destroyed the town of Paradise on November 8, 2018, killing 85 people and destroying 18,804 structures.

properties.name: Attica Wildfires
properties.description: Deadly wildfire swept through the seaside village of Mati near Athens on July 23, 2018. Rapid spread trapped residents trying to escape to the sea.

properties.name: Pedrógão Grande Fire
properties.description: Deadliest wildfire in Portuguese history. Many victims were trapped in their cars on a road between Figueiró dos Vinhos and Castanheira de Pêra on June 17, 2017.

properties.name: October 2017 Portugal Wildfires
properties.description: Series of devastating wildfires across central and northern Portugal in October 2017, fueled by Hurricane Ophelia's dry winds. Combined with the June fires, 2017 was Portugal's deadliest fire year.

properties.name: Fort McMurray Wildfire (The Beast)
properties.description: Costliest disaster in Canadian history (~$9.9 billion). Forced evacuation of all 88,000 residents of Fort McMurray in May 2016 and destroyed 2,400 structures.

properties.name: 2015 Indonesian Forest Fires
properties.description: Widespread peat and forest fires caused by El Niño drought and land-clearing practices. Generated a toxic haze that blanketed Southeast Asia for months.

properties.name: 2014 Northwest Territories Fires
properties.description: Record-breaking fire season in the Northwest Territories with over 385 fires burning 3.4 million hectares of boreal forest.

properties.name: 2010 Russian Wildfires
properties.description: Record heatwave fueled hundreds of wildfires across western Russia. Moscow was blanketed in thick smoke for weeks. Contributed to an estimated 56,000 excess deaths from heat and smoke.

properties.name: Black Saturday Bushfires
properties.description: Deadliest bushfire disaster in Australian history. On February 7, 2009, temperatures exceeded 46°C with extreme winds, igniting multiple fires that destroyed 2,029 homes.
properties.source: CFA Victoria / 2009 Victorian Bushfires Royal Commission

properties.name: 2007 Greek Forest Fires
properties.description: Series of devastating fires across Greece in August 2007, the worst in Greek history at the time. The Peloponnese was hardest hit with entire villages destroyed.

properties.name: 2003 Canberra Bushfires
properties.description: Bushfires reached the western suburbs of Canberra on January 18, 2003, destroying nearly 500 homes. A rare fire-generated tornado was observed.

properties.name: 2000 Montana Fire Season
properties.description: One of the worst US fire seasons in 50 years. Over 8.4 million acres burned nationally, with Montana and neighboring states bearing the brunt.

properties.name: 1997-98 Indonesian/Borneo Fires
properties.description: Catastrophic fires during the 1997–98 El Niño, burning massive areas of peatland and rainforest. Haze engulfed Southeast Asia, causing health and economic crises.

properties.name: Oakland Hills Firestorm
properties.description: The Tunnel Fire killed 25 people and destroyed over 3,000 homes in the Oakland and Berkeley hills on October 20, 1991, making it one of the worst urban-wildland fires in US history.

properties.name: Yellowstone Fires
properties.description: Largest fire complex in Yellowstone's history. Nearly 36% of the park burned in a series of fires from June through November 1988, transforming US fire management policy.

properties.name: Ash Wednesday Bushfires
properties.description: Over 180 fires broke out across Victoria and South Australia on February 16, 1983 in extreme conditions. One of Australia's deadliest bushfire events before Black Saturday.

properties.name: Black Dragon Fire
properties.description: Massive wildfire in northeastern China and into the Soviet Union in May 1987. One of the largest fires of the 20th century, burning 18 million acres of boreal forest.

properties.name: Sycamore Fire
properties.description: Fast-moving wildfire driven by Sundowner winds burned into the city of Santa Barbara in July 1977, destroying over 200 homes.

properties.name: Rio Grande do Sul Floods
properties.description: Worst flooding disaster in the state's history. Persistent heavy rains from late April through May 2024 submerged Porto Alegre and surrounding areas, displacing over 600,000 people.

properties.name: UAE / Dubai Floods
properties.description: Record rainfall of 254mm in 24 hours hit the UAE on April 16, 2024 — the heaviest in 75 years. Dubai's airport was flooded and roads became rivers.

properties.name: Libya Derna Floods (Storm Daniel)
properties.description: Storm Daniel brought extreme rainfall to eastern Libya on September 10, 2023. Two dams above Derna collapsed, sending a wall of water through the city and washing entire neighborhoods into the sea.

properties.name: 2022 Pakistan Floods
properties.description: Unprecedented monsoon rains and glacial melt flooded a third of Pakistan from June to October 2022, displacing 33 million people. Attributed partly to climate change.

properties.name: Western European Floods
properties.severity: Severe — deadliest European flood in decades
properties.description: Intense rainfall from storm Bernd caused catastrophic flooding in western Germany and eastern Belgium on July 14–15, 2021. The Ahr Valley was devastated with entire towns destroyed.

properties.name: Henan Province Floods
properties.severity: Extreme — year's worth of rain in 3 days
properties.description: Record-breaking rainfall hit Zhengzhou and Henan province in July 2021, with 201.9mm falling in a single hour. The Zhengzhou metro was flooded, trapping passengers.

properties.name: 2020 China Yangtze River Floods
properties.region: Yangtze River Basin, Hubei, Jiangxi, Anhui
properties.description: Prolonged heavy monsoon rains caused severe flooding along the Yangtze River from June to August 2020, affecting 63 million people and causing $25 billion in damage.

properties.name: 2019 Iran Floods
properties.description: Massive flooding across Iran from March to April 2019 after record rainfall. Flash floods devastated cities including Shiraz, and floodwaters persisted for weeks in Khuzestan.

properties.name: Kerala Floods
properties.severity: Worst in a century — all 14 districts affected
properties.description: Unusually heavy monsoon rainfall and dam releases caused catastrophic flooding across Kerala in August 2018, the worst in the state since 1924. Over 1.4 million people displaced.

properties.name: 2017 South Asian Floods (Bangladesh)
properties.description: Monsoon flooding in 2017 submerged roughly a third of Bangladesh, affecting over 8 million people. Simultaneous flooding hit Nepal and India's Bihar and Assam states.

properties.name: 2016 Louisiana Floods
properties.description: Slow-moving low-pressure system dropped up to 31 inches of rain over the Baton Rouge area in August 2016, flooding 146,000 homes. Called the worst US disaster since Hurricane Sandy.

properties.name: Tbilisi Flood
properties.description: Flash flooding and mudslides along the Vere River devastated parts of Tbilisi on June 13–14, 2015. The Tbilisi Zoo was destroyed and many animals escaped into the streets.

properties.name: Kashmir Floods
properties.description: Heavy monsoon rains caused devastating floods across Jammu & Kashmir in September 2014. Srinagar was inundated, with the Jhelum River reaching record levels.

properties.name: Uttarakhand Flash Floods
properties.description: Catastrophic flash floods and landslides struck the Uttarakhand region on June 16–17, 2013 during monsoon season. The Hindu pilgrimage site of Kedarnath was devastated.

properties.name: 2011 Thailand Floods
properties.region: Central Thailand, Chao Phraya River Basin, Bangkok
properties.description: Worst flooding in Thailand since 1942. Monsoon rains flooded the Chao Phraya River basin from July to January 2012, inundating industrial estates and parts of Bangkok. Cost $46 billion.

properties.name: 2010 Pakistan Floods
properties.region: Indus River Basin — KP, Punjab, Sindh, Balochistan
properties.description: Exceptional monsoon rains caused the Indus River to flood along almost its entire length. Approximately 20 million people were affected across 20% of Pakistan's land area.

properties.name: 2010-11 Queensland Floods
properties.severity: Severe — 3/4 of Queensland declared disaster zone
properties.description: Intense La Niña-driven rainfall caused widespread flooding across Queensland from December 2010 to January 2011. Flash floods devastated Toowoomba, and the Brisbane River flooded the CBD.

properties.name: 2005 Mumbai Floods
properties.description: Mumbai received 944mm of rainfall in 24 hours on July 26, 2005, one of the highest ever recorded in India. The city was paralyzed for days with massive loss of life and property.

properties.name: 1998 China Yangtze River Floods
properties.region: Yangtze River Basin, Hubei, Hunan, Jiangxi
properties.description: Massive flooding along the Yangtze River during the 1998 monsoon season affected 223 million people. Attributed to heavy rains combined with deforestation and wetland loss.

properties.name: Great Flood of 1993
properties.region: Mississippi and Missouri River Basins, Midwest
properties.description: Among the most costly and devastating floods in US history. The Mississippi and Missouri rivers flooded across nine states from April to October 1993, covering 30,000 square miles.

properties.name: 1991 Bangladesh Cyclone Flood
properties.description: Cyclone 02B made landfall on April 29, 1991, generating a 6-meter storm surge that inundated Bangladesh's southeastern coast, killing approximately 138,000 people.

properties.name: 2002 European Floods
properties.description: Extreme rainfall caused catastrophic flooding along the Elbe and Danube rivers in August 2002, devastating Prague, Dresden, and other cities. Damage exceeded €20 billion.

properties.name: 2000 Mozambique Floods
properties.description: Cyclone Eline and weeks of heavy rain caused massive flooding across southern Mozambique in February–March 2000, displacing hundreds of thousands. Images of helicopter rescues drew global attention.

properties.name: 1988 Bangladesh Floods
properties.description: Monsoon flooding submerged roughly 60% of Bangladesh from August to September 1988, including much of the capital Dhaka. Over 30 million people were affected.

properties.name: Typhoon Nina / Banqiao Dam Failure
properties.description: Typhoon Nina stalled over Henan Province in August 1975, dropping a year's worth of rain in 24 hours. The Banqiao Dam and 62 other dams failed, releasing a massive flood wave.

properties.name: Hurricane Harvey Floods
properties.severity: Catastrophic — 60+ inches of rain in some areas
properties.description: Hurricane Harvey stalled over the Houston area from August 25–30, 2017, dumping over 60 inches of rain in some areas. One of the costliest US natural disasters at $125 billion.

properties.name: Hurricane Mitch Floods
properties.description: Hurricane Mitch stalled off Honduras in October 1998, dumping torrential rain that caused catastrophic flooding and mudslides. Deadliest Atlantic hurricane since 1780 at the time.

properties.name: 2024 Amazon Drought
properties.severity: Worst recorded — rivers at historic lows
properties.description: Extreme drought dried up rivers across the Amazon basin in late 2024, stranding communities, killing river dolphins, and fueling record wildfires. The Rio Negro at Manaus fell to its lowest level ever recorded.

properties.name: Horn of Africa Drought (2020-2023)
properties.severity: Worst in 40 years — 5 consecutive failed rainy seasons
properties.description: Five consecutive failed rainy seasons from 2020 to 2023 created the worst drought in 40 years across the Horn of Africa. An estimated 43,000 excess deaths in Somalia alone in 2022.

properties.name: 2022 European Drought
properties.description: Europe experienced its worst drought in at least 500 years in the summer of 2022. Rivers dried up, crop yields fell sharply, and energy production was curtailed across the continent.

properties.name: Madagascar Drought
properties.description: Severe drought in southern Madagascar from 2020 to 2021 left over 1 million people food insecure, with some areas approaching famine. The first climate-change-driven famine according to some reports.

properties.name: 2019 India Drought
properties.severity: Severe — delayed monsoon, Chennai water crisis
properties.description: Delayed monsoon and below-normal rainfall led to severe drought across much of India in 2019. Chennai nearly ran out of water, and reservoirs across Maharashtra hit critically low levels.

properties.name: Cape Town Water Crisis
properties.description: Three years of below-average rainfall brought Cape Town close to 'Day Zero' — when taps would run dry — in early 2018. Strict water rationing of 50 liters per person per day was imposed.

properties.name: 2015-16 Southern Africa Drought
properties.country: South Africa / Zimbabwe / Malawi / Zambia / Mozambique
properties.severity: Worst in 35 years — El Niño driven
properties.description: Strong El Niño caused the worst drought in southern Africa since 1982-83. Over 40 million people faced food insecurity across the region. Maize production fell drastically.

properties.name: 2015-16 Ethiopian Drought
properties.region: Northern and Eastern Ethiopia (Tigray, Amhara, Oromia, Somali)
properties.severity: Worst in 50 years — 10+ million people food insecure
properties.description: El Niño-driven drought caused crop failures across Ethiopia in 2015-16. Over 10 million people required emergency food assistance, the country's worst food crisis in decades.

properties.name: 2012 United States Drought
properties.region: Central US — Great Plains, Midwest, South
properties.severity: Worst since 1956 — 65% of contiguous US in drought
properties.description: The most extensive drought in the US since the 1950s. By July 2012, 65% of the contiguous US was in drought. Massive crop losses led to $30 billion in agricultural damage.

properties.name: 2011 East Africa Drought
properties.severity: Worst in 60 years — famine declared in Somalia
properties.description: Severe drought caused a food crisis affecting over 13 million people. Famine was declared in parts of southern Somalia — the first famine declaration anywhere in the world since 1992.

properties.name: 2010 Amazon Drought
properties.severity: Worst on record at the time
properties.description: The 2010 Amazon drought was more severe than the previous record in 2005. The Rio Negro at Manaus fell to its lowest level in over a century, and millions of fish died.

properties.name: Millennium Drought (Australia)
properties.description: The Millennium Drought (1997–2009) was the worst drought in Australia's recorded history. 2006 was the peak year, with the Murray-Darling Basin experiencing its lowest inflows on record.

properties.name: 2002 India Drought
properties.region: Rajasthan, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Andhra Pradesh
properties.description: India experienced its worst drought in 15 years due to a major monsoon failure in 2002. Over 300 million people were affected, with severe impacts on agriculture and water supply.

properties.name: 1998 Southern US Drought
properties.description: Severe drought and heatwave gripped the southern United States in 1998, causing massive agricultural losses and contributing to over 200 heat-related deaths in Texas alone.

properties.name: 1991-92 Southern Africa Drought
properties.country: Zimbabwe / Mozambique / Zambia / South Africa / Malawi
properties.severity: Worst in 20th century for the region
properties.description: El Niño-driven drought devastated southern Africa in 1991–92. Maize production fell by 60% across the region, and over 20 million people faced severe food shortages.

properties.name: 1983-85 Ethiopian Famine Drought
properties.description: Prolonged drought from 1983 to 1985 caused one of the worst famines in modern history. An estimated 400,000 to 1 million people died. Prompted the Live Aid concert and global aid response.

properties.name: 1988 US Drought
properties.severity: Worst since Dust Bowl — $40 billion damage
properties.description: Severe drought across the Great Plains and Midwest in 1988 caused massive crop failures and water shortages. Yellowstone fires burned during this drought. Damage estimated at $40 billion.

properties.name: 1976 European Drought
properties.description: The summer of 1976 brought an exceptional drought to Western Europe, particularly the UK and France. Rivers dried up, crops failed, and the UK appointed a Minister for Drought.

properties.name: Afghanistan Drought
properties.description: Severe multi-year drought from 1998 to 2001 devastated Afghanistan's agriculture and water supply, displacing millions and compounding humanitarian crisis alongside conflict.

properties.name: 2014-15 Southeast Brazil Drought
properties.region: São Paulo, Minas Gerais, Rio de Janeiro
properties.severity: Worst in 80 years — São Paulo water crisis
properties.description: Severe drought struck southeastern Brazil from 2014 to 2015, threatening São Paulo's water supply for its 20 million residents. The Cantareira reservoir system fell below 5% capacity.

properties.name: Storm Daniel Greece Floods
properties.severity: Worst in Greek history — year's rain in hours
properties.description: Storm Daniel brought record rainfall to Thessaly in September 2023 before moving to Libya. Over 750mm fell in 18 hours, flooding the entire Thessaly plain and destroying crops.

properties.name: 2007 United Kingdom Floods
properties.description: Prolonged heavy rainfall in June and July 2007 caused widespread flooding across England, particularly in Yorkshire, the Midlands, and Gloucestershire. Over 55,000 properties were flooded.

properties.name: Vargas Tragedy Floods
properties.severity: Catastrophic — worst natural disaster in Venezuelan history
properties.description: Torrential rains in December 1999 triggered massive landslides and flash floods along Venezuela's Caribbean coast, destroying entire towns. Up to 30,000 people may have died.

properties.name: Hunga Tonga-Hunga Ha'apai Eruption
properties.description: Massive submarine eruption that generated a tsunami and atmospheric shockwave circling the globe multiple times. One of the most powerful eruptions recorded by modern instruments.

properties.name: Cumbre Vieja Eruption
properties.description: Eruption on La Palma island lasting 85 days, destroying over 2,900 buildings and displacing thousands. Lava flows reached the sea.

properties.name: Kilauea Lower East Rift Zone Eruption
properties.description: Major eruption with fissures opening in Leilani Estates subdivision. Lava destroyed over 700 homes and reshaped the coastline.

properties.name: Volcán de Fuego Eruption
properties.description: Devastating pyroclastic flows buried the village of San Miguel Los Lotes. Deadliest eruption in Guatemala in over a century.

properties.name: Mount Ontake Eruption
properties.description: Sudden phreatic eruption caught hikers by surprise on a popular autumn day. Deadliest volcanic disaster in Japan since 1926.

properties.name: Puyehue-Cordón Caulle Eruption
properties.description: Eruption sent ash plumes across South America and disrupted air travel in the Southern Hemisphere for months.

properties.name: Eyjafjallajökull Eruption
properties.description: Eruption produced enormous ash cloud that shut down European airspace for weeks, stranding millions of passengers worldwide.

properties.name: Mount Merapi Eruption
properties.description: Eruption with pyroclastic flows and lahars prompted evacuations of thousands near Yogyakarta. Part of ongoing Merapi activity cycle.

properties.name: Mount Nyiragongo Eruption
properties.description: Fast-moving lava flows from flank fissures inundated parts of Goma city, displacing 400,000 people and destroying 13% of the city.

properties.name: Hekla Eruption
properties.description: Brief but intense eruption producing a 15 km ash plume and lava flows. Aircraft were diverted from the area.

properties.name: Soufrière Hills Eruption (1997 Peak)
properties.description: Pyroclastic flows on June 25 killed 19 people and destroyed Plymouth, the capital. Two-thirds of the island was declared an exclusion zone.

properties.name: Soufrière Hills Eruption Onset
properties.description: Start of the long-duration eruption that would eventually bury Plymouth. Initial phreatic explosions led to evacuation of southern Montserrat.

properties.name: Mayon Volcano Eruption
properties.description: Pyroclastic flows killed 77 people, many of whom were farmers who had returned to the danger zone prematurely.

properties.name: Mount Pinatubo Eruption
properties.description: Second-largest eruption of the 20th century. Ejected 10 cubic km of material, lowered global temperatures by 0.5°C for two years.

properties.name: Nevado del Ruiz Eruption
properties.description: Eruption melted glacial ice creating massive lahars that buried the town of Armero. One of the deadliest volcanic disasters in history.

properties.name: El Chichón Eruption
properties.description: Three explosive eruptions destroyed the summit and several villages. Aerosol cloud lowered global temperatures by about 0.3°C.

properties.name: Mount St. Helens Eruption
properties.description: Catastrophic lateral blast removed the north face of the mountain. Largest landslide in recorded history accompanied the eruption.

properties.name: Sulawesi Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.description: M7.5 earthquake triggered a devastating tsunami in Palu Bay. Liquefaction destroyed entire neighborhoods.

properties.name: Tōhoku Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.description: M9.1 megathrust earthquake generated a massive tsunami devastating the northeast coast of Japan and triggering the Fukushima nuclear disaster.

properties.name: Chile Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.severity: Wave height up to 29 m locally
properties.description: M8.8 earthquake off Maule generated a Pacific-wide tsunami. Coastal towns in Chile were severely damaged.

properties.name: Samoa Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.description: M8.1 earthquake generated a tsunami that struck Samoa and American Samoa within minutes, devastating coastal villages.

properties.name: Java Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.description: M7.7 earthquake south of Java generated a tsunami that struck the southern coast, devastating the Pangandaran area.

properties.name: Indian Ocean Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.country: Indonesia, Sri Lanka, India, Thailand, Somalia, Maldives
properties.description: M9.1 megathrust earthquake off Sumatra generated the deadliest tsunami in recorded history, affecting 14 countries across the Indian Ocean.

properties.name: Papua New Guinea Tsunami
properties.description: M7.0 earthquake triggered an underwater landslide generating a devastating local tsunami that destroyed Sissano Lagoon villages.

properties.name: Okushiri Island Tsunami
properties.description: M7.8 earthquake in the Sea of Japan generated a tsunami that struck Okushiri Island within minutes, devastating the small fishing town of Aonae.

properties.name: Nicaragua Tsunami
properties.description: Slow 'tsunami earthquake' (M7.7) generated disproportionately large waves along the Nicaraguan coast despite weak ground shaking.

properties.name: Sunda Strait Tsunami (Anak Krakatau)
properties.description: Flank collapse of Anak Krakatau volcano during eruption generated a tsunami without seismic warning, striking western Java and southern Sumatra.

properties.name: Greenfield Iowa Tornado
properties.description: Violent tornado struck the small town of Greenfield, Iowa, destroying much of the downtown area and killing five residents.

properties.name: Rolling Fork Mississippi Tornado
properties.description: Powerful nighttime tornado devastated the town of Rolling Fork, Mississippi, leveling entire blocks.

properties.name: December 2021 Kentucky Tornado Outbreak
properties.description: Long-track supercell produced a tornado traveling over 165 miles, devastating Mayfield, KY and other communities across multiple states.

properties.name: Mayfield Kentucky Tornado
properties.description: Tornado destroyed the Mayfield Consumer Products candle factory and much of downtown Mayfield during December outbreak.

properties.name: Dayton Ohio Tornado Outbreak
properties.description: Powerful late-night tornado tore through Dayton suburbs as part of a broader outbreak across the Miami Valley.

properties.name: Kokomo Indiana Tornado
properties.description: Series of tornadoes struck central Indiana, with the strongest hitting Kokomo and causing significant damage to homes and a Starbucks.

properties.name: 2013 Moore Oklahoma Tornado
properties.description: Violent EF5 tornado carved a 17-mile path through Moore, destroying two elementary schools and thousands of structures. Winds estimated at 210 mph.

properties.name: 2013 El Reno Oklahoma Tornado
properties.description: Widest tornado ever recorded at 2.6 miles wide with 295 mph subvortex winds. Killed storm chasers Tim Samaras and his team.

properties.name: Joplin Missouri Tornado
properties.description: Deadliest US tornado since 1947. EF5 tornado carved a mile-wide path through Joplin, destroying St. John's hospital and thousands of buildings.

properties.name: 2011 Super Outbreak (April 27)
properties.description: Largest tornado outbreak in history with 362 confirmed tornadoes in a single 24-hour period, including four EF5 tornadoes across the Southeast.

properties.name: Tuscaloosa-Birmingham Tornado
properties.description: Massive tornado cut a mile-wide path through Tuscaloosa and Birmingham during the April 27 Super Outbreak.

properties.name: Super Tuesday Tornado Outbreak
properties.description: Major outbreak on February 5-6 produced 87 tornadoes across the Southeast, with the deadliest tornadoes striking Tennessee.

properties.name: Greensburg Kansas Tornado
properties.description: EF5 tornado with 205 mph winds destroyed 95% of the town of Greensburg. The town later rebuilt as a model green community.

properties.name: Bridge Creek-Moore Tornado
properties.description: Produced the highest wind speed ever recorded on Earth at 302 mph (DOW radar). Destroyed 8,000 homes in the Oklahoma City metro area.

properties.name: 1999 Oklahoma City Tornado Outbreak
properties.description: Outbreak produced 66 tornadoes on May 3, including the Bridge Creek-Moore F5 and several other violent tornadoes across central Oklahoma and Kansas.

properties.name: 2024 South Asia Heatwave
properties.severity: Temperatures exceeding 50°C in parts of India and Pakistan
properties.description: Extreme heatwave with temperatures above 50°C in Rajasthan and Sindh. Widespread heat-related deaths and water shortages across the subcontinent.

properties.name: 2023 Southern European Heatwave (Cerberus/Charon)
properties.description: Back-to-back heatwaves named Cerberus and Charon broke temperature records across southern Europe. Greece experienced devastating wildfires.

properties.name: 2022 European Heatwave
properties.severity: UK record 40.3°C; France, Spain, Portugal exceeded 45°C
properties.description: Record-breaking heat across Western Europe. UK exceeded 40°C for the first time. Over 60,000 heat-related deaths estimated across the continent.

properties.name: 2021 Pacific Northwest Heat Dome
properties.description: Unprecedented heat dome shattered all-time records. Lytton, BC set Canada's all-time record at 49.6°C before being destroyed by wildfire the next day.

properties.name: 2020 Siberian Heatwave
properties.description: Prolonged warmth across Siberia reached 38°C in Verkhoyansk, the highest temperature ever recorded above the Arctic Circle. Triggered massive wildfires and permafrost thaw.

properties.name: 2019 European Heatwaves (June/July)
properties.severity: France record 46°C; records broken in Germany, Belgium, Netherlands
properties.description: Two successive heatwaves in June and July broke national all-time records across Western Europe. France hit 46°C for the first time.

properties.name: 2018 Japan Heatwave
properties.description: Deadly heatwave following historic floods. Kumagaya set Japan's all-time record at 41.1°C. Over 1,000 deaths and 50,000+ hospitalizations.

properties.name: 2017 Lucifer Heatwave
properties.severity: Temperatures up to 46°C in southern Italy
properties.description: Heatwave nicknamed 'Lucifer' brought extreme temperatures across southern Europe, fueling wildfires in Italy, Croatia, and Greece.

properties.name: 2015 India-Pakistan Heatwave
properties.description: Devastating heatwave killed over 2,500 in India and 1,600 in Pakistan. Karachi alone suffered over 1,200 deaths in a single week.

properties.name: 2013 Australian Heatwave (Angry Summer)
properties.severity: National average record 40.3°C; new color added to weather maps
properties.description: Extreme heat so intense that the Bureau of Meteorology added new colors to weather maps. National average temperature set a record at 40.3°C.

properties.name: 2012 North American Heatwave
properties.severity: Hottest year on record for contiguous US at the time
properties.description: Record-breaking heat across the central and eastern US. 2012 became the hottest year on record for the contiguous United States. Severe drought accompanied the heat.

properties.name: 2010 Russian Heatwave
properties.description: Worst heatwave in Russian history. Moscow recorded 38.2°C as peat and forest fires blanketed the city in toxic smog. Estimated 55,000 excess deaths.

properties.name: 2006 European Heatwave
properties.severity: UK record 36.5°C; widespread over 40°C on continent
properties.description: July heatwave brought scorching temperatures across Western Europe, with hundreds of excess deaths in France, the Netherlands and the UK.

properties.name: 2003 European Heatwave
properties.country: France, Italy, Spain, Portugal, Germany, UK
properties.severity: France exceeded 44°C; hottest European summer in 500 years
properties.description: Deadliest European natural disaster in decades. France alone lost nearly 15,000 people. Hottest European summer in at least 500 years according to paleoclimate data.

properties.name: Winter Storm Uri (Cold Wave)
properties.severity: Temperatures -19°C in Dallas; coldest in Texas since 1899
properties.description: Historic Arctic outbreak brought record cold to Texas and the Deep South, collapsing the power grid and leaving millions without heat for days.

properties.name: 2019 Polar Vortex Cold Wave
properties.severity: Wind chill -56°C in Minnesota; Chicago -30°C actual
properties.description: Polar vortex displacement brought the coldest temperatures in a generation to the Upper Midwest. Chicago recorded -30°C, colder than parts of Antarctica.

properties.name: Beast from the East
properties.country: United Kingdom, Ireland, France, Netherlands, Germany
properties.severity: UK temperatures below -10°C; heavy snowfall across Europe
properties.description: Sudden stratospheric warming event displaced polar vortex, driving Siberian air across Europe. Heavy snow paralyzed the UK and Ireland.

properties.name: 2014 North American Polar Vortex
properties.severity: Wind chills below -50°C; record lows across Midwest and East
properties.description: Polar vortex disruption brought Arctic air deep into the Eastern US in early January. All 50 states experienced below-freezing temperatures.

properties.name: 2012 European Cold Wave
properties.country: Ukraine, Poland, Romania, Russia, Italy, France
properties.description: Severe cold wave in late January and February killed hundreds across Europe, particularly homeless populations in Eastern European countries.

properties.name: 2010 European Cold Wave
properties.severity: UK temperatures below -20°C; heaviest December snow in decades
properties.description: Extreme cold and heavy snowfall in late November through December paralyzed transportation across Europe. UK experienced coldest December in 100 years.

properties.name: 1985 United States Cold Wave
properties.severity: Record lows below -40°C; Florida citrus devastated
properties.description: Extreme Arctic outbreak in January sent temperatures plunging across the Eastern US. Florida's citrus industry suffered over $1 billion in damage.

properties.name: 1983 United States Cold Wave
properties.severity: Record lows; wind chills below -60°C in Midwest
properties.description: December 1983 cold wave brought record-breaking cold to much of the US. Over 270 deaths attributed to the extreme temperatures.

properties.name: 2022 Buffalo Blizzard (Elliott)
properties.severity: 125 cm snow; winds 110 km/h; whiteout conditions for 36+ hours
properties.description: Historic Christmas blizzard trapped residents in vehicles and homes for days. Lake-effect snow combined with an Arctic cyclone to paralyze Buffalo.

properties.name: Blizzard Jonas (Snowzilla)
properties.severity: Up to 107 cm snow; hurricane-force gusts on coast
properties.description: Massive nor'easter buried the Mid-Atlantic and Northeast. New York City received its second-largest snowfall on record. Widespread coastal flooding.

properties.name: Blizzard Juno
properties.severity: Up to 91 cm snow; blizzard warnings for 29 million people
properties.description: Powerful nor'easter brought heavy snow and strong winds to New England. Travel bans implemented in New York and New England states.

properties.name: 2014 Atlanta Ice Storm / Southern Blizzard
properties.severity: 5-8 cm ice/snow paralyzing Southeast; 10,000+ vehicles stranded
properties.description: Light snow and ice brought the Atlanta metropolitan area to a standstill. Thousands of motorists were stranded on frozen highways for over 24 hours.

properties.name: Snowmageddon (February 2010)
properties.severity: Up to 100 cm snow; back-to-back storms
properties.description: Two major blizzards struck the Mid-Atlantic within a week, burying Washington DC under record snowfall. Federal government closed for a full week.

properties.name: 2008 Afghanistan Blizzard
properties.severity: Temperatures below -30°C; extreme snowfall in Hindu Kush
properties.description: Extreme cold wave and blizzards killed nearly 1,000 people across Afghanistan. Livestock losses devastating to rural communities.

properties.name: 1996 North American Blizzard
properties.severity: Up to 120 cm snow; record snowfall in multiple cities
properties.description: Massive blizzard in January paralyzed the Eastern Seaboard from Virginia to New England. Followed by severe flooding during rapid thaw.

properties.name: 1993 Storm of the Century
properties.severity: Up to 140 cm snow; hurricane-force winds; record low pressures
properties.description: Superstorm combined characteristics of a blizzard, hurricane, and tornado outbreak. Affected the entire Eastern Seaboard from Cuba to Canada.

properties.name: 1978 Northeastern United States Blizzard
properties.severity: Up to 69 cm snow; winds 175 km/h; 10-foot storm surge
properties.description: Devastating nor'easter paralyzed New England for over a week. Rhode Island declared a state of emergency as coastal flooding and snow buried communities.

properties.name: Winter Storm Uri Ice Storm
properties.severity: Up to 2.5 cm ice accumulation; mass power grid failure
properties.description: Ice storm component of Winter Storm Uri caused catastrophic ice accumulation across Texas, collapsing power infrastructure and leaving 4.5 million without electricity.

properties.name: 2023 Tennessee/Southeast Ice Storm
properties.description: Significant ice storm across Tennessee and the Southeast caused widespread power outages and hazardous travel. Over 100,000 customers lost power.

properties.name: 2013 Central US Ice Storm
properties.description: Major ice storm across Kansas, Oklahoma, and Missouri in December. Downed trees and power lines left hundreds of thousands without electricity.

properties.name: 2009 Kentucky/Arkansas Ice Storm
properties.severity: Up to 5 cm ice accumulation; worst in Kentucky history
properties.description: Catastrophic ice storm left 1.3 million without power across Kentucky and Arkansas. Some areas went without electricity for weeks in frigid conditions.

properties.name: 1998 North American Ice Storm
properties.severity: Up to 10 cm ice accumulation; worst in Canadian history
properties.description: Most destructive ice storm in Canadian history. Over 4 million lost power in Quebec and Ontario. Electrical pylons collapsed under massive ice loads. 35 deaths across the region.

properties.name: 1995 Chicago Heatwave
properties.severity: Heat index up to 54°C; temperatures over 41°C
properties.description: Five-day heatwave killed over 700 people in Chicago alone. Disproportionately affected elderly and low-income residents without air conditioning.
